reviews.review_description:
The graphic style was interesting, like a comic style.
Sounds are ok.. from the voices, to the famous Tarzan shout.

It’s a story based game, each comic giving a new mission/story.
You can find unlocks along your way and weapons to select and use.
Smash coconuts to eat and pick bananas.. to regain strength.

There’s running sections, swinging sections which are implemented very well, and fighting sections with fists or weapons.

I think I this is aimed more at children with its ease of gameplay.

Still an interesting one though.

The iconic Tarzan cry will stay with me forever, such a wow moment as I grabbed my first vine and swung from tree to tree.
Cartoon style gameplay does not distract from what the devs are trying to bring us, some good old fashioned platform fun.
As an older title it plays well for its age, don’t take it too serious and just enjoy it for what it is.
A-a-a-a-ah-ah-ah-ah-ah

This is a simple, fun walk through nostalgic comic books. Controls are simple and intuitive, the scenes are reminiscent of the old cartoons and the tasks are straightforward and fun. No significant challenges, but a fun walk through memory lane. Fun and suitable for younger players. You jump right into the game ‘treehouse’ area without much of an introduction, but it is fairly straightforward.  There are options to change movement style with the left menu button if you struggle with motion sickness

So I was fully immersed in things such as vine-swinging, swimming, climbing, and combat across various episodes.  Sounds were great and graphically it was ok, bright and vibrant.  It doesn’t appear over challenging, I may enjoy it for a short time but I feel kids will enjoy it a lot more especially due to the simplistic gameplay and a short campaign.
What a brilliant game sound graphics really cool

This game has awesome boss fights, intense vine swinging levels, and offers a great storyline. It is older, but still worth playing, the graphics work amazingly and you feel as though you are in the jungle. Players are able to spend time collecting quest objects and exploring the jungle as well!! It can be fun to discuss where these objects may be over on the FunTrain discord!

Stayed away from this game for a long time because of the reviews. When it went on sale for $6, I figured it was time to try. Nearly returned it because the running did not work for me nor did the swinging. After several attempts, I figured out what to do when the swinging didn’t work and was able to get through the entire game in less than 2 hours. So it doesn’t work well at times and is really short, but it was a decent little adventure and I was ok with it for what I paid.
Graphics suck I advise you people to not get this game because it is just the worst it can’t get fun just for a little bit but then it’s boring so no not at this game
I wanted to get multiplayer what feels like 69 years￼

developer_response.body: We have considered mutiplayer, but there can only be ONE Tarzan!

reviews.review_description:
Gameplay: https://youtu.be/Vl-5dglx9vk

This whole game has a brand new update. New graphics and swinging with extras. I hadnt played it before but what i see now i enjoyed, Only played map one so far its not perfect the fighting could be better but adding all the tarzan audio is a great feature.

The lowered the price also which is great as it was too expensive before. Its a fun 3 level game for a price of a cinema ticket go check it out.

Tarzan was given a right good spanking when it came out and rightly so. After a huge update and a price drop almost a year later. Is it worth your time?

Well yes. The game is quite fun and does look good now. Combat isn't the best and swinging is pretty straight forward but the discovery of the Apes as you progress through the game is always fun.
The drop in price is a big factor and I used a discount code so only paid £5 for it.

Downside is still the length. I was quite excited to see 3 chapters to play and thought I had a good few hours of play ahead of me. It might be that I was having fun and didn't notice the time,but felt I done everything in about 90mins.

Graphics have had a huge update but the last level was still a bit hit or miss. Seemed to be rendering at a slower pace than I was walking and everything was grey. But when they were fine,they looked nice.


Overall I'm happy I played through it for the price I paid.

It is just a pity that the quest store isn't the best for game update news and it will be easily overlooked that this has had an update 👍

I did not play the game before the update, but now the graphics level is acceptable if we do not take into account certain failures in the union of the polygons of the environment and the excesive fog, although it is far from the psvr version (I can't understand). The fun for money is good.

Question for the developers: Why doesn't it include spanish language and the pc version does?
When they include it I will give my 5 stars. Thanks for your work.

developer_response.body:
Hey mr. Rugarso.
Thanks for the feedback! We will be looking into that Spanish translation.
Cheers

reviews.review_title: I want to like this/ now I do
reviews.review_description:
Edit 2- Everything I had a problem with has been fixed. It took some time but it's now a great game. I think just a little more communication about updates would have helped. But I strongly recommend the game now. If you refunded it then buy it again. Great game. 


EDIT 1- No update and not one response from a developer. Just thrown on the store and left. We as the VR community expect better. Minus 1 more star. 

This game is amazing on PC. I don't expect the Quest version to equal the PC. So far the only downside is no menu to change settings. I do not like snap turn but I assume it will be added later. I will support this game because I know it's potential. Will update when smooth turning is added. But I'm sure updates will come after feedback from players.
Took a year but now actually fun to play.  👍🏻Worth buying again if you refunded like me.
I played the first chapter for a hour because it's hard, and I can not beat it

developer_response.body: We've heard you!   Please make sure you download the new update.

reviews.review_description:
It has all three episodes.   Art style is super cool, and totally dig the soundtrack.  

This game is really nice for your more intermediate to beginner VR player.  Its friendly for younger audiences/teens as well and that might be where it hits the sweet spot.  If you or someone else you know has a craving for a variety story driven game, this hits the spot.

Its nice to see things like real story in a game as well as humor.  Is the the most intense game ever? no - but it is a solid way to get that Tarzan feeling that is only possible in VR.  Its nice to see the devs went above and beyond too with bringing elements like voice acting, real characters.  It also has a ton of weapons and variety, way more than to be expected.  The chance to feel like you are swinging from vines, in the sea and more is fantastic.

Honestly this is complete trash. It’s 2022 guys. This trash is not acceptable and to charge for this is a blemish on your CV. You will have no reputation to work with if you do this again. Embarrassing

developer_response.body:
Hey Paul, have you checked the game again after our last update?
I think you might enjoy it much more now!

reviews.review_description: Tarzan Vr is awesome game and i play it sometimes every single day and this is my favorite vr game because its Tarzan and Tarzan is my favorite Disney Movie and In My opinion Tarzan Vr is a awesome Vr Game to experience Tarzan The Lord of the jungle

reviews.review_title: I WISH I WOULD HAVE LISTENED...TERRIBLE
reviews.review_description:
Please I say again please DO NOT BUY!!!!! I've purchased atleast 30 games from the store and this is my only review so far this game is absolute TRASH....PLEASE SAVE YOUR MONEY

YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED

reviews.review_title: Don’t waste your money, or your time.
reviews.review_description:
Possibly one of the worst releases on the Oculus store. Can’t imagine how this got here when better titles linger in App Lab limbo. Sure, the Tarzan name carries weight, but this game is an utter disappointment. Can’t believe it’s being released at full price.

See our full review at https://youtu.be/CKKFB34k1zo or search “Tarzan 6DOF Reviews”..

I believe the bad reviews are due to one single thing: expectations. 
Don't expect a port for quest to be as pretty as the other platforms. 

It's still the same game, same fun and easy mechanics. Just don't expect complex combat and open world exploration. 

It's fun, it's stylish, it's a nice experience.

reviews.review_title: Sadly inferior to the PC version.
reviews.review_description:
Impressions Video with Gameplay in a REAL FOREST:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KLkitQSWhUs

My Initial Impressions:
Im a BIG fan of Tarzan in VR, but sadly this version on Quest 2 is not a patch on the PC version. Textures are bad, I had performance issues and the draw distance is terrible and kills the immersion. Combat is laughable and Im sad to report this is a bad port of a great game.
If you have a PC and VR play the PC version, its feels like a better game.

reviews.review_title: I was actually excited for this..
reviews.review_description:
Can't believe how poorly put together this is, I played through the first comic in 5 minutes.
The physics, characters environments and swing mechanic is all bad and for 18.99? I've played better games for free! 

Will 100% get a refund.

reviews.review_title: Very fun once you get a hang of it
reviews.review_description: Pretty fun swinging from branches and watching monke dialogue

Total dumpster fire. Third level won’t load. Slingshot is too weak, fists are wayyyyyyy too weak. The first 2 levels only lasted 57 minutes. THIS GAME IS THE EQUIVALENT OF A DUMPSTER FIRE IN A VOLCANO, IN THE SUMMER, ON THE SUN, FILLED WITH GASOLINE AND SEMI TRUCKS
Poop
I thought the game would be good but its really really bad

Having played the entire game at this point, I enjoyed it. The swinging worked well once I learned the mechanics and when you have a long stretch of vines it’s a lot of fun to zip through the canopy and takedown an enemy from the trees. 

The game is a challenge, and requires you to master blocking paired with quick jabs in hand to hand combat - and even then it’s sometimes difficult to get through the areas where there are a lot of enemies. 

The longbow in issue 3 is by far the best weapon, and the boss fights in that issue are also the most enjoyable. My favorite part is probably the audio from old Tarzan clips scattered throughout the game. 

The critiques have some merit, the brightness seemed a bit low, the hand to hand combat could use some tweaking and the physics are not ultra-realistic, however, I enjoyed it through those day 1 flaws. I definitely want to play the Steam version now as well, just to experience the higher res textures - but it’s always nice having wireless as an option. I think the younger people in my family would have a blast with it. 

The 3 issues took me about 3 hours playing at my usual relaxed pace. I did get stuck in one area on issue 2 for a while which extended that time but overall I was happy with the length of the gameplay.

developer_response.body: Make sure you're using the block feature when engaging in hand-to-hand combat- it could save your life!

reviews.review_description: This game is great for my young nieces and nephews, it's like living in comic book. The game is simple, fun, and easy to understand and there is no blood and gore. The swinging is fun and the spiders will start to make the primitive part of your brain scream run.

This game is super fun! I love the blend of retro comic art and old school radio samples with the stylish atmosphere and action-style gameplay. It's a quest game where I find myself getting swept up in the swinging and fighting and forget to pause and explore in the jungle. Looking forward to a repeat play with my kids. Does anyone know what happens when you collect all the hidden records?

Different people have different likings but if you like simple and cartoons or comics it is great but if you are expecting something more long and complicated it is not for you. I myself love the experience.

developer_response.body: Tarzan was one of the first comic book heroes. We're glad you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description: Me and my kids played this for hours at a friend's party and totally loved it! I'm into the world-building and the great Tarzan throwbacks -- my kids are in to the action and the vine swinging. I've been looking for a game that bridges my childhood with theirs. Excited to fire this back up again!

developer_response.body: We're so happy to introduce a new generation of fans to Tarzan.

reviews.review_description: Love the vine swinging in this game! Probably my favorite part... until I discovered the ocean swimming. But watch out for that croc!

developer_response.body: We're glad you're enjoying it. If you see a croc in the water- Swim fast in the other direction!

reviews.review_title: I’ve played much better games for free
reviews.review_description:
🚫FIRST IMPRESSIONS/GAMEPLAY/REVIEW🚫
Just search ‘Tarzan VR Gamertag VR’ on YouTube 

https://youtu.be/Hqsccxs183g
This is a fun game. Swinging on the vines is really cool, especially once you get the rhythm down and know that you can pull up to launch you to the next vine.
At first I thought it would be cool to have something where u can swing and fight things but the game is horrible. I wanted to like it but I just couldn’t

reviews.review_title: Fun game especially for the kiddos
reviews.review_description: My son likes it and i find it occupying. Very cute premise and reminds me of my childhood
I hate it so much

I don’t normally leave reviews, but almost didn’t purchase based upon 1 star ratings from the trolls. Glad I didn’t listen. Had a blast swinging through the jungle and knocking out the bad guys.  Cool retro vibe, love the comic book art style, and feels like there’s lots to explore.

Breakdown of the game,

GRAPHICS - The cell shading and colors gave the world a nice living comic book feel. The jungle looked amazing and immersive. The character modes of the Gorillas and animals you fight look good. Giving you that comic book world feel. Some of the humans though, just didn't feel like the same art style and out of place.

SOUND - The sounds effects and music I would say was average. The character lines / voices were good. One thing I enjoyed was the random background lines from Tarzan. It was like someone was reading the comic book thought bubbles to me. Helped with the comic book world feel.

CONTROL - Moving Tarzan around felt smooth and great. Climbing trees, jumping and basic movement are spot on. Combat was great. Weapons and punches feel like they are connecting, and you can tell when you laid a punch or not. Swinging from vines feels good, but can still use some work. There are a few times it was hard to get the next vine. Interacting with the world is coming along nice. It has a gravity grab system, so you don't need to bend to pick anything up. The aiming of it was easy. There are a few items I had a hard time picking up. Its like they were stuck in the map.

FUN FACTOR - As someone who grew up playing Tarzan and reading the books, this was a really fun VR adventure. I really enjoyed being Tarzan, and being in his world. All you 40+ gamers who grew up with Tarzan will want this one.

IMMERSION - From the opening scene to the VR world. You feel like you are in a comic book. The graphics, voices, and world all set you up for the comic book dive. I specially loved the feeling of running and jumping of a ledge into the water below.
